microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's **recovery validation program** from a 2-person operation into a platformized system, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a scalable, repeatable model.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new continuous-validation standard.

Served as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud **network isolation test** tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract, directing 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility across cloud-native and distributed infrastructure environments.

Built an internal **AI agent** to automate drill planning workflows, reducing planning cycle time by 39% and increasing drill capacity by 21% — enabling the team to scale technical engagements without adding headcount.
microsoft_2023: Championed adoption of **AI-driven code generation** workflows by conducting user demos and training sessions across 14 Azure service teams, saving 37 engineering hours monthly and accelerating developer productivity at scale.

Influenced the product roadmap to include **intent-based YAML generation** by facilitating 11+ user interviews with Azure service teams, surfacing critical feature gaps and translating technical feedback into prioritized requirements.

Improved data integrity and retrieval by transitioning documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** tool with rigorous metadata standards, cutting lookup time by 83% and demonstrating clear, measurable value to engineering stakeholders.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched Azure regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework spanning 140+ engineering and cloud-infrastructure teams.

Conducted discovery with 20+ service teams to identify **81 hours of manual effort** per region launch, then built a roadmap to automate critical paths — directly reducing operational toil across cloud-native deployment workflows.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** to track automation gaps across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to surface high-impact prioritization decisions and align technical resources to the most critical infrastructure bottlenecks.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and coordinating execution across IT, Operations, and Engineering.

Drove strategic alignment across cross-functional teams to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and reducing integration risk across a large-scale, distributed robotics infrastructure.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilize the developer intake process under tight delivery timelines.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by optimizing the end-to-end scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals, implementing a new CRM process that improved sales-cycle efficiency and customer conversion rates.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and boosted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the website and Google profile to drive measurable pipeline growth.

Improved leadership pricing models by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, identifying and sealing margin leaks that directly strengthened per-job revenue performance.

tailoring_notes: JD term 'proof-of-value': woven into microsoft_ft bullet 1 and bullet 3 via outcome framing showing measurable technical validation delivered to enterprise customers — mirrors SE POV language directly.

JD term 'cloud-native technologies' and 'API Gateways/microservices': surfaced in microsoft_ft bullet 4 ('cloud-native and distributed infrastructure') and microsoft_2022 bullet 2 ('cloud-native deployment workflows'), and promoted to top of skills_priority section.

JD term 'network isolation' and sovereign/enterprise technical credibility: microsoft_ft bullet 4 explicitly references 'network isolation test' tied to a major enterprise contract, directly paralleling Salt's enterprise security sales motion and customer trust-building emphasis.

JD emphasis on 'technical subject matter expertise' and earning customer trust: microsoft_ft bullets 3–5 reframed to highlight deep hands-on technical delivery (rack-level drills, AI agents, cross-org execution) rather than pure program management, matching SE credibility requirements.

JD term 'Kubernetes, AWS, Azure, Load Balancers': all four surfaced in skills_priority row 2; microsoft_ft and amazon_robotics bullets reference distributed infrastructure and cloud environments to substantiate hands-on familiarity expected by the JD.
